posts._template: NEWS / ANNOUNCEMENT — Edit or duplicate this block for announcements
posts.title: AFOLU Ethiopia Signs New Partnership with Regional Universities
posts.author: AFOLU Communications Office
posts.body: AFOLU of Ethiopia has signed a memorandum of understanding with three regional universities to strengthen research and capacity building in agriculture, forestry, and land use. The partnership will support student placements, joint research projects, and knowledge exchange programmes starting in mid-2026.

posts._template: EVENT — Edit or duplicate this block for events
posts.title: National Conference on Food Security and Climate Resilience — May 2026
posts.author: AFOLU Events Secretariat
posts.body: We are hosting a two-day national conference on Food Security and Climate Resilience on 14–15 May 2026 in Finfinnee. The conference will bring together government bodies, development partners, researchers, and farmers to share findings and shape national policy. Registration opens 15 April 2026. Contact afoluofethiopia@gmail.com to participate.

posts._template: REPORT / PUBLICATION — Edit or duplicate this block for reports and publications
posts.title: Annual AFOLU Sector Report 2025 Now Available
posts.author: Research & Documentation Unit
posts.body: The AFOLU Ethiopia Annual Sector Report 2025 has been published and is available for download in the Reports & Publications section. The report covers key performance indicators across agriculture, forestry, and land use for the fiscal year, including data on reforestation targets, crop yields, and carbon stock assessments.

posts._template: WEBSITE CONTENT CHANGE — Edit or duplicate this block to announce site updates
posts.title: Website Updated — New Geospatial & GIS Section Added
posts.author: AFOLU Web Team
posts.body: Our website has been updated with a new Geospatial & GIS section featuring interactive land-use maps, satellite imagery analyses, and downloadable shapefiles for Ethiopia's major agro-ecological zones. Visit the Geospatial & GIS section from the navigation menu to explore the new resources.

posts._template: EXTRA EXAMPLE — Delete this block when you are ready to publish
posts.title: Green Legacy Initiative Reaches 10 Billion Trees Milestone
posts.author: Green Legacy Secretariat
posts.body: Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative has officially reached the 10 billion trees planted milestone, marking a historic achievement in national reforestation and environmental restoration. AFOLU Ethiopia played a key coordination role in achieving this target across all regional states.
